text_segment: Your body belongs to you. No one has the right to touch you in ways that make you uncomfortable. You can always say no to touches that do not feel right.
question: Explain who your body belongs to.
Active: My body belongs to me alone.
Progressive: I am protecting my body every day.
Passive: My body is owned only by me.
Subordinate: Because my body belongs to me, I decide who can touch it.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: because,my body belongs to me,,,i decide who,can touch it,.

text_segment: You always have the right to say no to any touch that makes you uncomfortable. Even if the person is a grown-up or someone you know, you can still say no. Your feelings matter.
question: Explain your right to say no.
Active: I can say no to uncomfortable touches.
Progressive: I am learning to say no when needed.
Passive: No can be said by me at any time.
Subordinate: Even if someone is older, I can still say no.

order_subordinate_beginner_1: even if,someone is older,,,i can still,say no,.

text_segment: Private parts are the parts of your body covered by a bathing suit. These areas are private and personal. Only doctors during checkups with a parent present should need to see them.

Active: Private parts are covered by a bathing suit.
Progressive: I am keeping my private areas private.
Passive: Private areas are protected by me.
Subordinate: Because they are private, only doctors with parents may check them.

text_segment: Safe touches feel comfortable, like a hug from a family member. Unsafe touches make you feel scared, confused, or uncomfortable. Trust your feelings about touches.

Progressive: I am trusting my feelings about touches.
Passive: Unsafe touches are recognized by how they make me feel.
Subordinate: If a touch feels wrong, it probably is wrong.
order_passive_beginner_1: unsafe touches,are recognized,by how they,make me feel,.

text_segment: If someone makes you feel unsafe, try to get away. You can walk away, run to a safe place, or go to where other people are. Getting away is always okay.

Active: I can get away from unsafe situations.
Progressive: I am moving toward a safe place.
Passive: Unsafe situations can be left by me.
Subordinate: When I feel unsafe, I try to get away.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: when,i feel unsafe,,,i try to,get away,.

text_segment: Always tell a trusted adult if something makes you feel unsafe. Keep telling until someone helps you. It is never your fault, and you will not get in trouble for telling.

Progressive: I am telling someone I trust right away.
Passive: Unsafe things should be told to a trusted adult.

order_passive_beginner_1: unsafe things,should be told,to a,trusted adult,.
order_subordinate_beginner_1: until,someone helps me,,,i keep,telling,.

text_segment: A trusted adult is someone who keeps you safe and listens to you. This might be a parent, teacher, school counselor, or relative. Everyone should have several trusted adults they can talk to.

order_subordinate_beginner_1: because,trusted adults care about me,,,they will,help,.

text_segment: Good surprises are fun and will be shared soon, like a birthday party. Bad secrets make you feel worried or scared and someone says never to tell. You should always tell an adult about bad secrets.

text_segment: If someone hurts you or makes you feel unsafe, it is never your fault. Adults are supposed to protect children, not hurt them. You deserve to be safe and cared for.

text_segment: Your body gives you warning feelings when something is wrong. You might feel scared, nervous, or get a funny feeling in your stomach. These feelings are telling you to be careful.

Passive: Warning signs are given by my body.

text_segment: Some unsafe people use tricks to confuse children. They might offer gifts, say it is a special secret, or say you will get in trouble. These are tricks, and you should tell a trusted adult.

text_segment: Unsafe people might try to bribe you with gifts or threaten to hurt you or someone you love. These are not okay. A trusted adult will protect you and help you stay safe.

text_segment: Sometimes unsafe people try to become special friends with children before doing something wrong. They give lots of attention and gifts. If an adult makes you feel uncomfortable, tell someone you trust.

text_segment: Never share personal information online. This includes your full name, address, school, phone number, or passwords. Strangers online could use this information to find you.
